feed.title: SAP Community - ABAP Development
feed.subtitle: ABAP Development Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Clean core enhancement for ML81n when saving
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I have a requirement to implement an enhancement in ML81n when a user clicks save when creating a service entry sheet.

I am currently using customer exit EXIT_SAPLMLSR_001 (SRVEDIT) but I am unable to restrict my code to only trigger on the save button as fields such as sy-ucomm, ok_save etc. are cleared by this point.

I need to do some validation after the save button is clicked with the SES cost centre entered vs the linked PO cost centre and send out an email.

The client is quite strict about following clean core principles, therefore, I am unable to use implicit enhancements such as in include LMLSRF0F.

Please assist with a BAdI/Customer Exit for this use case or some method of detecting the user clicked 'save' to restrict my current code.

Client is on Private Cloud.

entry.title: SAP note required by itself? Question about SAP Support Web site
entry.content:
I can find some SAP notes with the prerequisite that the SAP note requires "itself".

Is it a bug, or does it mean something?

Thanks.

Sandra

For instance, 3428737 - CVI_VAL: When deleting contact last name desired error "Maintain req. field Last Name in contact person BP &" not raised - SAP for Me.

entry.title: Replicate the QR Code to custom Smartform, decoding from Table EDOPLINV
entry.content:
Hi All,

How to replicate the QR Code, for an Invoice that was accepted by the KSeF, e-invoicing System and sent back the PDF with QR Code?  The QR Code (not the pdf)  is stored in the Table Field EDOPLINV-QRCODE_ACCEPTED.

Expected Result: 
The QR Code should be re-generated in a custom Smart form, from the value stored in the Table field EDOPLINV-QRCODE_ACCEPTED. when scanned, it should re-direct to KSef PL Gov website.

Procedure:
I have the following test code written in the Smart form, very similar to this blog post QR Code in Base64 encoding for KSA E-Invoicing - Directly from data in Table EDOSAINV  
Note: In the- above Blog, QR Code is  encoded to Base 64, But I don't want this.

  1. Configured System Bar Code in SE73 for QR Code with "QR Code 2005".
  2. Created a smart form style, For a  Character font, assigned the QR Code system bar code to it.
  3. Assigned the custom style to my custom Smart form.
  4. Below is the code written in the Initialization of Smart form (Please note this is a prototype)
*Global Data
 Data:
 EV_QRCODE type string. "or xstring
SELECT SINGLE
  FROM edoplinv
FIELDS *
 WHERE edoc_guid = '699D630206330553E1000000A467BBA9'
  INTO ls_edoplinv.

  DATA lv_image_base64 TYPE string.
  DATA lo_xstr_to_str_conv TYPE REF TO cl_abap_conv_in_ce.
  cl_abap_conv_in_ce=>create(
    EXPORTING
      encoding = 'UTF-8'
      input    = ls_edoplinv-qr_code_accepted
    RECEIVING
      conv     = lo_xstr_to_str_conv
  ).
  lo_xstr_to_str_conv->read( IMPORTING data = lv_image_base64 ).

  DATA: lv_image_xstring TYPE xstring.
  lv_image_xstring = cl_http_utility=>decode_x_base64( 
                         encoded = lv_image_base64 ).

*--Assign to the QRCode variable used in the UI
   EV_QRCODE = lv_image_xstring

In the smart form, created a text with the following snippet

<QR>&EV_QRCODE&</>​

The QR Code is generated, but does not point to the KSeF, instead shows a 4 digit gibberish value.

Expected URL starts with below URL
https://qr-demo.ksef.mf.gov/client-app/invoice/

 Can you please suggest how to fix it ?

entry.title: BAPI_SALESORDER_CHANGE returns success message but does not take effect.
entry.content:
I am trying to use the BAPI_SALESORDER_CHANGE inside MV45AFZZ and SE37 but the changes does not reflect on the documents.

Even though there was a message that the Sales Document was saved, the changes does not reflect when i check it in VA03 or SE16.

DATA: ls_order_header_in  TYPE bapisdh1,
      ls_order_header_inx TYPE bapisdh1x,
      lt_return           TYPE TABLE OF bapiret2.
CLEAR: 
     ls_order_header_in, 
     ls_order_header_inx, 
     lt_return.

IF vbak-auart = 'ZVCC'.

SELECT 
       vbeln 
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_vbfa)
  FROM vbfa
  WHERE vbelv = vbak-vbeln
    AND vbtyp_n = 'C'.

IF lt_vbfa IS NOT INITIAL.

" Some contract have related multiple sales doc
LOOP AT lt_vbfa INTO DATA(ls_vbfa).
  ls_order_header_in-incotermsv = vbak-incov.
  ls_order_header_inx-updateflag = 'U'.
  ls_order_header_inx-incotermsv = abap_true.

  CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_SALESORDER_CHANGE'
    EXPORTING
      salesdocument    = ls_vbfa-vbeln
      order_header_in  = ls_order_header_in
      order_header_inx = ls_order_header_inx
    TABLES
      return           = lt_return.
ENDLOOP.
ENDIF.
ENDIF.

entry.title: Failed to resolve navigation target "#Bank-manageExtended"
entry.content:
Hi all,

I extended the Manage Banks App F1574 using an adaptation project, and when I open the app, it displays the following error message:

Failed to resolve navigation target "#Bank-manageExtended". This is most likely caused by an incorrect SAP Fiori launchpad content configuration or by a missing role assignment.

Here is the target mapping and the tile configuration:

 

The content of the manifest.appdescr_variant

{
  "fileName": "manifest",
  "layer": "CUSTOMER_BASE",
  "fileType": "appdescr_variant",
  "reference": "fin.cash.bankmaster.manage",
  "id": "customer.customer.fin.cash.bankmaster.manage.extension",
  "namespace": "apps/fin.cash.bankmaster.manage/appVariants/customer.customer.fin.cash.bankmaster.manage.extension/",
  "content": [
    {
      "changeType": "appdescr_app_setTitle",
      "content": {},
      "texts": {
        "i18n": "i18n/i18n.properties"
      }
    },
    {
      "changeType": "appdescr_ui5_addNewModelEnhanceWith",
      "content": {
        "modelId": "i18n",
        "bundleUrl": "i18n/i18n.properties",
        "supportedLocales": [
          ""
        ],
        "fallbackLocale": ""
      }
    },
    {
      "changeType": "appdescr_ui5_addNewModelEnhanceWith",
      "content": {
        "modelId": "@i18n",
        "bundleUrl": "i18n/i18n.properties",
        "supportedLocales": [
          ""
        ],
        "fallbackLocale": ""
      }
    },
    {
      "changeType": "appdescr_ui5_setMinUI5Version",
      "content": {
        "minUI5Version": "1.108.23"
      }
    }
  ]
}

and /sap/bc/ui2/app_index/ui5_app_info?id=customer.customer.fin.cash.bankmaster.manage.extension

{
  "customer.customer.fin.cash.bankmaster.manage.extension": {
    "dependencies": [
      {
        "id": "customer.customer.fin.cash.bankmaster.manage.extension",
        "componentId": "fin.cash.bankmaster.manage",
        "type": "UI5COMP",
        "url": "/sap/bc/ui5_ui5/sap/fin_bank",
        "descriptorUrl": "/sap/bc/lrep/content/~20251209123247.9324920~/apps/fin.cash.bankmaster.manage/appVariants/customer.customer.fin.cash.bankmaster.manage.extension/manifest.appdescr_variant",
        "error": false,
        "noDescriptor": false
      },
      {
        "id": "sap.ui.table",
        "componentId": "sap.ui.table",
        "type": "UI5LIB",
        "url": "",
        "descriptorUrl": "",
        "error": false,
        "noDescriptor": false
      },
      {
        "id": "sap.suite.ui.commons",
        "componentId": "sap.suite.ui.commons",
        "type": "UI5LIB",
        "url": "",
        "descriptorUrl": "",
        "error": false,
        "noDescriptor": false
      },
      {
        "id": "sap.fe.placeholder",
        "componentId": "sap.fe.placeholder",
        "type": "UI5LIB",
        "url": "",
        "descriptorUrl": "",
        "error": false,
        "noDescriptor": false
      },
      {
        "id": "sap.ui.core",
        "componentId": "sap.ui.core",
        "type": "UI5LIB",
        "url": "",
        "descriptorUrl": "",
        "error": false,
        "noDescriptor": false
      },
      {
        "id": "sap.ui.generic.app",
        "componentId": "sap.ui.generic.app",
        "type": "UI5LIB",
        "url": "",
        "descriptorUrl": "",
        "error": false,
        "noDescriptor": false
      },
      {
        "id": "sap.ui.comp",
        "componentId": "sap.ui.comp",
        "type": "UI5LIB",
        "url": "",
        "descriptorUrl": "",
        "error": false,
        "noDescriptor": false
      },
      {
        "id": "sap.m",
        "componentId": "sap.m",
        "type": "UI5LIB",
        "url": "",
        "descriptorUrl": "",
        "error": false,
        "noDescriptor": false
      },
      {
        "id": "sap.ui.layout",
        "componentId": "sap.ui.layout",
        "type": "UI5LIB",
        "url": "",
        "descriptorUrl": "",
        "error": false,
        "noDescriptor": false
      },
      {
        "id": "/sap/opu/odata/sap/YBMD_FCLM_BM_SRV/",
        "componentId": "",
        "type": "ODATA",
        "typeVersion": "2.0",
        "url": "",
        "descriptorUrl": "",
        "error": false,
        "noDescriptor": false
      },
      {
        "id": "sap.uxap",
        "componentId": "sap.uxap",
        "type": "UI5LIB",
        "url": "",
        "descriptorUrl": "",
        "error": false,
        "noDescriptor": false
      },
      {
        "id": "annotation.xml",
        "componentId": "",
        "type": "ODATAANNO",
        "typeVersion": "2.0",
        "url": "",
        "descriptorUrl": "",
        "error": false,
        "noDescriptor": false
      },
      {
        "id": "sap.f",
        "componentId": "sap.f",
        "type": "UI5LIB",
        "url": "",
        "descriptorUrl": "",
        "error": false,
        "noDescriptor": false
      },
      {
        "id": "sap.suite.ui.generic.template",
        "componentId": "sap.suite.ui.generic.template",
        "type": "UI5LIB",
        "url": "",
        "descriptorUrl": "",
        "error": false,
        "noDescriptor": false
      },
      {
        "id": "sap.fin.central.lib",
        "componentId": "sap.fin.central.lib",
        "type": "UI5LIB",
        "url": "/sap/bc/ui5_ui5/sap/fin_lib",
        "descriptorUrl": "/sap/bc/lrep/content/~20251209123227.4211770~/apps/sap.fin.central.lib/app/sap/fin_lib/manifest.appdescr",
        "error": false,
        "noDescriptor": false
      },
      {
        "id": "sap.se.mi.plm.lib.attachmentservice",
        "componentId": "sap.se.mi.plm.lib.attachmentservice",
        "type": "UI5LIB",
        "url": "/sap/bc/ui5_ui5/sap/plm_ath_cres1",
        "descriptorUrl": "/sap/bc/lrep/content/~20251209123311.4398020~/apps/sap.se.mi.plm.lib.attachmentservice/app/sap/plm_ath_cres1/manifest.appdescr",
        "error": false,
        "noDescriptor": false
      },
      {
        "id": "/sap/opu/odata/sap/CV_ATTACHMENT_SRV/",
        "componentId": "",
        "type": "ODATA",
        "typeVersion": "2.0",
        "url": "",
        "descriptorUrl": "",
        "error": false,
        "noDescriptor": false
      },
      {
        "id": "sap.s4h.cfnd.featuretoggle",
        "componentId": "sap.s4h.cfnd.featuretoggle",
        "type": "UI5LIB",
        "url": "/sap/bc/ui5_ui5/sap/featuretoggles1",
        "descriptorUrl": "/sap/bc/lrep/content/~20251209123229.0314360~/apps/sap.s4h.cfnd.featuretoggle/app/sap/featuretoggles1/manifest.appdescr",
        "error": false,
        "noDescriptor": false
      },
      {
        "id": "sap.collaboration",
        "componentId": "sap.collaboration",
        "type": "UI5LIB",
        "url": "",
        "descriptorUrl": "",
        "error": false,
        "noDescriptor": false
      },
      {
        "id": "sap.ushell",
        "componentId": "sap.ushell",
        "type": "UI5LIB",
        "url": "",
        "descriptorUrl": "",
        "error": false,
        "noDescriptor": false
      },
      {
        "id": "/sap/opu/odata/SAP/CA_FM_FEATURE_TOGGLE_STATUS_SRV",
        "componentId": "",
        "type": "ODATA",
        "typeVersion": "2.0",
        "url": "",
        "descriptorUrl": "",
        "error": false,
        "noDescriptor": false
      },
      {
        "id": "customer.customer.fin.cash.bankmaster.manage.extension",
        "componentId": "",
        "type": "APPVARID",
        "url": "/sap/bc/lrep/flex/data/~a9jzRjGlwBHbsAUULWgdfQ+2JAU=~/customer.customer.fin.cash.bankmaster.manage.extension",
        "descriptorUrl": "",
        "error": false,
        "noDescriptor": false
      },
      {
        "id": "sap.ui.fl",
        "componentId": "sap.ui.fl",
        "type": "UI5LIB",
        "url": "",
        "descriptorUrl": "",
        "error": false,
        "noDescriptor": false
      }
    ],
    "error": false,
    "noDescriptor": false
  }
}

What am I doing wrong?

Best regards

entry.title: How to pass longtext via extraction enable CDS to Datasphere
entry.content:
I need to pass longtext in datasphere using extraction enabled CDS. However, neither longtext nor table functions can be used for it.
Could you please to identify the best way to do it.

Regards,
Malika Bathla.

SAP Datasphere SAP S/4HANA Embedded Analytics SAP S/4HANA

entry.title: High importance flag in outlook from ABAP for email using SO_DOCUMENT_SEND_API
entry.content: I have a requirement to show high importance flag in outlook which means showing red exclamation in  the outlook mail. In my abap code we are using SO_DOCUMENT_SEND_API1 , we have tried to set the priority in document_data  even then we didn't receive the high priority email with red exclamation in the email. How can i find solution for this?

entry.title: Reference Equipment or Copy Option Not Visible While Creating Equipment
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

We are trying to create equipment using the Reference Equipment option. However, in our system the Reference Equipment or Copy option is not visible while creating the equipment.

Because of this, we are unable to create new equipment using an existing equipment as a reference.

Advance Thanks

entry.title: BAPI_PO_CREATE1 PO not selectable in MIGO – missing Conditions tab vs ME21N
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I am facing an issue with purchase orders created viaBAPI_PO_CREATE1.

When I create a PO manually using ME21N, I can post a Goods Receipt in MIGO without any problem.
However, when I create a PO using BAPI_PO_CREATE1, I get the following error in MIGO:

“Document 450000XXXX does not contain any selectable items.”

What I have checked so far:

  • The PO created via ME21N and the PO created via BAPI have the same header and item data (material, plant, quantity, etc.).
  • The PO created via the BAPI is not delivery completed, not blocked, and fully released.
  • The issue only occurs for BAPI-created POs.

Key difference I noticed:

  • In the PO created via ME21N, the Conditions tab exists and pricing conditions (e.g. PB00 / PBXX) are present.
  • In the PO created via BAPI_PO_CREATE1, the Conditions tab is missing.

What I tried:

  • Populating POCOND / POCONDHEADER and their corresponding X structures (POCONDX, POCONDHEADERX)

However, the Conditions tab is still missing, and the PO items are not selectable in MIGO.

My question:

  1. Is there something specific that must be populated in BAPI_PO_CREATE1 to ensure pricing is finalized (similar to ME21N)?
  2. Are there mandatory fields or flags (e.g. pricing-related fields, CHANGE_ID, NET_PRICE, PRICE_UNIT, etc.) that are commonly missed when creating POs via the BAPI?

Any guidance or references to relevant SAP Notes would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you in advance!

entry.title: PO created via BAPI_PO_CREATE1 cannot be used for GR in MIGO, but ME21N PO works
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I am having an issue with purchase orders created using BAPI_PO_CREATE1.

If I create a PO manually with ME21N, I can post a goods receipt in MIGO without any issues. However, if I create a PO using BAPI_PO_CREATE1, I get the following error in MIGO when trying to post GR:

“Document 450000XXXX does not contain any selectable items.”

I compared a PO created via ME21N and one created via the BAPI. Header and item data such as material, plant, quantity, etc. are the same, and the BAPI-created PO is not blocked, not delivery completed, and fully released.

One clear difference I noticed is that the PO created via ME21N has a Conditions tab with pricing conditions maintained, while the PO created via BAPI_PO_CREATE1 does not have the Conditions tab at all.

I tried populating POCOND and POCONDHEADER (as well as the corresponding X structures) when calling the BAPI, but this did not change the result. The Conditions tab is still missing, and the PO items are still not selectable in MIGO.

My questions are:

  • Can missing or incomplete pricing/conditions cause PO items to be non‑selectable for goods receipt in MIGO?
  • Is there anything specific that needs to be populated in BAPI_PO_CREATE1 to ensure pricing is finalized in the same way as ME21N?
  • Are there any commonly missed mandatory fields or flags related to pricing (for example NET_PRICE, PRICE_UNIT, CHANGE_ID, etc.) when creating POs via the BAPI?

Any advice or pointers would be appreciated. Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Create multiple task lists with service packages, with CALL FUNCTION 'EAM_TASKLIST_CREATE'
entry.content:
Dear all,

I need a simple example how to create 2 task lists with service packages via fm  'EAM_TASKLIST_CREATE' inside a loop.

entry.title: Custom Container issue
entry.content:
I have created an ALV report which has 3 more ALVs as popups when double clicked on each when displayed, I have used SALV Factory and Custom Container for all the ALVs, and displaying the popups by passing the container objects to factory method of SALV, now for the very first popup which is after the standard ALV screen works fine when I close the popup and select new record, but when I double click on a pop it open a new alv popup and displays values, but when I close this popup and try to select new record from previous popup it still displays the old records. In debug mode I can see the internal table passed to factory method for these pop double clicks fetches correct data each time, but displays the records displayed the very first time

Can somebody help me with this as I have tried refreshing

entry.title: Superpackage ZCUSTOM_DEVELOPMENT is not a valid package
entry.content:
Dear Community,

Using the Developer Extensibility model, we created several custom objects. At the start of development, the structure package ZCUSTOM_DEVELOPMENT was not available in the TDD system (Partner Demo Development Tenant), so we used the structure package ZPARTNER and completed the development.

Now, while preparing to transport these objects to QA, we are encountering the error: “Software component of transport is inconsistent.” The ZPARTNER package currently has no transport layer assigned.

Since the ZCUSTOM_DEVELOPMENT structure package is now available, I attempted to move our development packages under it. However, this results in the error: “Superpackage ZCUSTOM_DEVELOPMENT is not a valid package.”

What options are available to move these objects to the QA system?

Regards,
Karthik

entry.title: How to call a custom FM once after all changed line items in VA01/VA02?
entry.content:
Hi all,

I’m working on VA01/VA02 and have a requirement:

  • The user can change the item category for multiple line items.

  • After the user edits the line items and presses Enter, I need to pass all the changed line items to a custom function module to show a popup.

  • The popup should be called only once, regardless of how many line items were changed.

  • I do not want the popup to wait until the user clicks Save.

I’ve tried using USEREXIT_CHECK_VBAP and USEREXIT_MOVE_FIELD_TO_VBAP, but the FM gets triggered multiple times (once per line item).

Is there a standard exit or best practice to detect “all line items have been changed” so I can call my FM only once before Save? Or is the memory table + flag approach the recommended solution?

Thanks in advance for any guidance!

Thanks and regards,

Praveen de Silva.

entry.title: Is SAP ABAP developer still a good career option in 2026 for someone starting fresh in IT?
entry.content:
Hi everyone,
I’m a 22-year-old Computer Science graduate currently navigating the transition from academia to the professional world. Over the past few months of job hunting, I’ve done some deep soul-searching regarding my technical strengths.
While I have a solid grasp of theory, system design, and "under-the-hood" fundamentals, I’ve realized that high-pressure, DSA-heavy competitive coding rounds aren't where I shine. I am a logical thinker who enjoys building structured, business-oriented solutions rather than solving abstract algorithmic puzzles.
This has led me to research the SAP Ecosystem, specifically ABAP, as a career path. I’m particularly drawn to the structured nature of enterprise software. However, I have a few questions for the experts here:
  1. Market Viability in 2026: For someone starting fresh today, is the demand for ABAP developers still strong, specifically with the industry-wide shift to S/4HANA?
  2. The AI Impact: How is SAP Joule and Generative AI changing the daily workflow of an ABAPer? Does a "Clean Core" strategy mean more or less work for custom developers?
  3. The "Full-Stack" SAP Dev: I see a lot of talk about SAP BTP, Fiori, and RAP (RESTful ABAP Programming). Should a fresher aim to be a "Pure ABAPer," or is it essential to start with a BTP-first mindset?
  4. Career Longevity: Coming from a non-competitive coding background, can I build a high-growth career here? Does the SAP world value system-design thinking over raw coding speed?
I’m eager to contribute to the enterprise space and want to ensure I’m investing my time in a future-proof stack,  I’d love to hear from those of you currently in the trenches.
Thank you for your time and guidance!

entry.title: HQ Logo Display Issue in Excel OLE2?
entry.content:
Hello, I’ve set up a structure like the one below. I have no issues creating the Excel file or using the methods listed below. I can successfully merge the Excel file as I want, fill it in, and perform tasks such as adding images to the Excel sheet.

However, despite not zooming in excessively and having a high-quality file on SE78, the image quality on the Excel sheet is degraded.

Has anyone else encountered this situation before?

REPORT zka_demo3.


CLASS gcl_excel DEFINITION.
  PUBLIC SECTION.
    CLASS-METHODS:

      excel_merge_cell IMPORTING iv_begrow TYPE i
                                 iv_endrow TYPE i OPTIONAL
                                 iv_begcol TYPE i
                                 iv_endcol TYPE i
                       CHANGING  io_excel  TYPE ole2_object,

      excel_fill_cell IMPORTING iv_row             TYPE i
                                iv_col             TYPE i
                                iv_bold            TYPE i OPTIONAL
                                iv_italic          TYPE i OPTIONAL
                                iv_val             TYPE string
                                iv_color           TYPE string OPTIONAL
                                iv_center          TYPE char1 OPTIONAL
                                iv_right_alignment TYPE char1 OPTIONAL
                                iv_border          TYPE char1 OPTIONAL
                                iv_border_only     TYPE char1 OPTIONAL
                                iv_left            TYPE char1 OPTIONAL
                                iv_right           TYPE char1 OPTIONAL
                                iv_top             TYPE char1 OPTIONAL
                                iv_bottom          TYPE char1 OPTIONAL
                                iv_autofit         TYPE char1 OPTIONAL
                                iv_font_size       TYPE i OPTIONAL
                                iv_font_color      TYPE i OPTIONAL
                                i_wraptext         TYPE char1 OPTIONAL
                      CHANGING  io_excel           TYPE ole2_object
                                io_zl              TYPE ole2_object OPTIONAL
                                io_f               TYPE ole2_object OPTIONAL
                                io_interior        TYPE ole2_object OPTIONAL,

      set_format   IMPORTING i_bgnrow TYPE i
                             i_bgncol TYPE i
                             i_endrow TYPE i
                             i_endcol TYPE i
                             i_fname  TYPE string OPTIONAL
                             i_fcolor TYPE i OPTIONAL
                             i_fbold  TYPE i OPTIONAL
                             i_fsize  TYPE dec10_2 OPTIONAL
                             i_csize  TYPE i OPTIONAL
                             i_fitlc  TYPE i OPTIONAL
                             i_fuline TYPE i OPTIONAL
                             i_bcolor TYPE i OPTIONAL
                             i_merge  TYPE char1 OPTIONAL
                             i_nfdec  TYPE char255 OPTIONAL
                   CHANGING  io_excel TYPE ole2_object,

      set_border IMPORTING i_bgnrow   TYPE i
                           i_bgncol   TYPE i
                           i_endrow   TYPE i
                           i_endcol   TYPE i
                           i_trbli    TYPE char5
                           i_wraptext TYPE char1 OPTIONAL
                           i_endcoll  TYPE char1 OPTIONAL
                           iv_left    TYPE char1 OPTIONAL
                           iv_right   TYPE char1 OPTIONAL
                           iv_center  TYPE char1 OPTIONAL
                 CHANGING  io_excel   TYPE ole2_object,

      set_line_border IMPORTING i_bgnrow  TYPE i
                                i_bgncol  TYPE i
                                i_endrow  TYPE i
                                i_endcol  TYPE i
                                i_trbli   TYPE char5
                                iv_left   TYPE char1 OPTIONAL
                                iv_right  TYPE char1 OPTIONAL
                                i_endcoll TYPE char1 OPTIONAL
                      CHANGING  io_excel  TYPE ole2_object,


      excel_logo IMPORTING iv_logo  TYPE  stxbitmaps-tdname OPTIONAL
                           iv_satir TYPE i OPTIONAL "Left
                           iv_sutun TYPE i  OPTIONAL "Top
                           iv_left  TYPE ole2_object OPTIONAL
                           iv_top   TYPE ole2_object OPTIONAL
                           iv_opt   TYPE char1 OPTIONAL
                 CHANGING  cv_sutun TYPE i OPTIONAL
                           co_sheet TYPE ole2_object
                           co_excel TYPE ole2_object,

ENDCLASS.

CLASS gcl_excel IMPLEMENTATION.

  METHOD excel_logo.

    TYPES :
      BEGIN OF ty_graphic ,
        line TYPE char255,
      END OF ty_graphic .

    DATA: l_bytecount TYPE i,
          lv_rc       TYPE i,
          l_rc        TYPE i,
          lv_content  TYPE xstring,
          gt_graphic  TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ty_graphic,
          l_content   TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bapiconten.

    DATA: graphic_size TYPE i.
    DATA: ws_filename  TYPE string VALUE 'C:\temp\logo.bmp'.
    DATA: l_image_path TYPE string          VALUE 'C:\temp\logo.bmp'.
    DATA: shapes TYPE ole2_object.
    DATA: lv_name TYPE  stxbitmaps-tdname.

    IF iv_logo IS NOT INITIAL.

      CALL FUNCTION 'SAPSCRIPT_GET_GRAPHIC_BDS'
        EXPORTING
          i_object       = 'GRAPHICS'
          i_name         = iv_logo
          i_id           = 'BMAP'
          i_btype        = 'BCOL'
        IMPORTING
          e_bytecount    = l_bytecount
        TABLES
          content        = l_content
        EXCEPTIONS
          not_found      = 1
          bds_get_failed = 2
          bds_no_content = 3
          OTHERS         = 4.

      IF sy-subrc NE 0.
        MESSAGE ID sy-msgid TYPE sy-msgty NUMBER sy-msgno
        WITH sy-msgv1 sy-msgv2 sy-msgv3 sy-msgv4.
      ENDIF.

      CALL FUNCTION 'SAPSCRIPT_CONVERT_BITMAP'
        EXPORTING
          old_format               = 'BDS'
          new_format               = 'BMP'
          bitmap_file_bytecount_in = l_bytecount
        IMPORTING
          bitmap_file_bytecount    = graphic_size
        TABLES
          bds_bitmap_file          = l_content
          bitmap_file              = gt_graphic
        EXCEPTIONS
          OTHERS                   = 1.

      CALL FUNCTION 'SCMS_BINARY_TO_XSTRING'
        EXPORTING
          input_length = 34565
        IMPORTING
          buffer       = lv_content
        TABLES
          binary_tab   = gt_graphic
        EXCEPTIONS
          failed       = 1
          OTHERS       = 2.
      IF sy-subrc <> 0.
        MESSAGE ID sy-msgid TYPE sy-msgty NUMBER sy-msgno
                   WITH sy-msgv1 sy-msgv2 sy-msgv3 sy-msgv4.
      ENDIF.

      CONCATENATE 'C:\temp\' iv_logo '.bmp' INTO ws_filename.

      CALL FUNCTION 'GUI_DOWNLOAD'
        EXPORTING
          filename                = ws_filename
          filetype                = 'BIN'
        TABLES
          data_tab                = gt_graphic
        EXCEPTIONS
          file_write_error        = 1
          no_batch                = 2
          gui_refuse_filetransfer = 3
          invalid_type            = 4
          no_authority            = 5
          unknown_error           = 6
          header_not_allowed      = 7
          separator_not_allowed   = 8
          filesize_not_allowed    = 9
          header_too_long         = 10
          dp_error_create         = 11
          dp_error_send           = 12
          dp_error_write          = 13
          unknown_dp_error        = 14
          access_denied           = 15
          dp_out_of_memory        = 16
          disk_full               = 17
          dp_timeout              = 18
          file_not_found          = 19
          dataprovider_exception  = 20
          control_flush_error     = 21
          OTHERS                  = 22.

      DATA:
        cl_fxs_image_info TYPE REF TO cl_fxs_image_info.

      cl_fxs_image_info = NEW #( ).

      cl_fxs_image_info->determine_info( EXPORTING iv_data = lv_content
        IMPORTING ev_mimetype = DATA(l_ev_mimetype)
                  ev_xres     = DATA(l_ev_xres)
                  ev_yres     = DATA(l_ev_yres)
                  ev_xdpi     = DATA(l_ev_xdpi)
                  ev_ydpi     = DATA(l_ev_ydpi)
                  ev_bitdepth = DATA(l_ev_bitdepth) ).


      GET PROPERTY OF  co_sheet 'Shapes' = shapes.

      IF iv_opt EQ 'X'. " If fixed
        l_ev_xres = '180'.
        l_ev_yres = '67'.
      ENDIF.

      IF iv_logo NE 'ZLOGO'. "SE78
        l_ev_xres = 180 * 75 / 100.
        l_ev_yres = 67 * 75 / 100.
      ENDIF.

      CALL METHOD OF shapes 'AddPicture'
        EXPORTING
          #1 = ws_filename "Image file on the presentation server
          #2 = 1
          #3 = 1
          #4 = iv_satir "Left
          #5 = iv_sutun "Top
          #6 = l_ev_xres "Width
          #7 = l_ev_yres. "Height


      cv_sutun = l_ev_xres.

      CALL METHOD cl_gui_frontend_services=>file_delete
        EXPORTING
          filename           = ws_filename
        CHANGING
          rc                 = l_rc
        EXCEPTIONS
          file_delete_failed = 1
          cntl_error         = 2
          OTHERS             = 3.
    ENDIF.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD excel_merge_cell.
    DATA: lo_cell1 TYPE ole2_object,
          lo_cell2 TYPE ole2_object,
          lo_cells TYPE ole2_object.

    DATA : lv_endrow TYPE i.

    IF iv_endrow IS INITIAL.
      lv_endrow = iv_begrow.
    ELSE.
      lv_endrow = iv_endrow.
    ENDIF.

    CALL METHOD OF io_excel 'Cells' = lo_cell1
     EXPORTING
     #1 = iv_begrow
     #2 = iv_begcol.
    CALL METHOD OF io_excel 'Cells' = lo_cell2
      EXPORTING
      #1 = iv_endrow
      #2 = iv_endcol.
    CALL METHOD OF io_excel 'Range' = lo_cells
      EXPORTING
      #1 = lo_cell1
      #2 = lo_cell2.

    CALL METHOD OF lo_cells 'Select'.
    CALL METHOD OF lo_cells 'Merge'.

  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD excel_fill_cell.

    DATA : ls_rows    TYPE ole2_object.

    CALL METHOD OF io_excel 'Cells' = io_zl
    EXPORTING
    #1 = iv_row
    #2 = iv_col.

    SET PROPERTY OF io_zl 'Value' = iv_val .
    GET PROPERTY OF io_zl 'Font' = io_f.

    IF iv_font_size IS NOT INITIAL.
      SET PROPERTY OF io_f 'Size' = iv_font_size.
      IF iv_font_color IS NOT INITIAL.
        SET PROPERTY OF io_f 'ColorIndex' = iv_font_color.
      ENDIF.
    ENDIF.

    SET PROPERTY OF io_f 'Bold' = iv_bold .
    SET PROPERTY OF io_f 'Italic' = iv_italic.

    IF iv_color IS NOT INITIAL.
*      For background
      GET PROPERTY OF io_zl 'Interior' = io_interior .
      SET PROPERTY OF io_interior 'ColorIndex' = iv_color.
*      SET PROPERTY OF lo_interior 'Color' = color.
    ENDIF.

    IF iv_center EQ 'X'.
      SET PROPERTY OF io_zl 'HorizontalAlignment' = 3.
    ENDIF.

    IF iv_left EQ 'X'.
      DATA(lv) = 5.
      SET PROPERTY OF io_zl 'HorizontalAlignment' = 2.
    ENDIF.

    "1 = default
    "2 = left
    "3 = center
    "4 = right
    "5 = justify

    IF iv_right_alignment EQ 'X'.
      SET PROPERTY OF io_zl 'HorizontalAlignment' = 4.
    ENDIF.

    IF iv_border EQ 'X' AND iv_border_only = 'X'.
      CALL METHOD OF io_zl 'BorderAround'
        EXPORTING
          #1 = 1 "'LineStyle' = '1'. "line style solid, dashed...
          #2 = 3. "'WEIGHT' = 4. "max = 4
    ENDIF.

    IF iv_autofit EQ 'X'.
      DATA: lo_auto TYPE ole2_object.
      GET PROPERTY OF io_zl 'COLUMNS' = lo_auto.
      SET PROPERTY OF lo_auto 'AutoFit' = 2.
    ENDIF.

    IF iv_border EQ 'X' .
      DATA: lo_border TYPE ole2_object.
      IF iv_left EQ 'X'.
        CALL METHOD OF io_zl 'BORDERS' = lo_border EXPORTING #1 = '1'.  "left
        SET PROPERTY OF lo_border 'LineStyle' = '1'. "line style solid, dashed...
        SET PROPERTY OF lo_border 'WEIGHT' = 3. "max = 4
        FREE OBJECT lo_border.
      ENDIF.

      IF iv_right EQ 'X'.
        CALL METHOD OF io_zl 'BORDERS' = lo_border EXPORTING #1 = '2'.  "right
        SET PROPERTY OF lo_border 'LineStyle' = '1'. "line style solid, dashed...
        SET PROPERTY OF lo_border 'WEIGHT' = 3. "max = 4
        FREE OBJECT lo_border.
      ENDIF.
      IF iv_top EQ 'X'.
        CALL METHOD OF io_zl 'BORDERS' = lo_border EXPORTING #1 = '3'.  "top
        SET PROPERTY OF lo_border 'LineStyle' = '1'. "line style solid, dashed...
        SET PROPERTY OF lo_border 'WEIGHT' = 3. "max = 4
        FREE OBJECT lo_border.
      ENDIF.
      IF iv_bottom EQ 'X'.
        CALL METHOD OF io_zl 'BORDERS' = lo_border EXPORTING #1 = '4'.  "bottom
        SET PROPERTY OF lo_border 'LineStyle' = '1'. "line style solid, dashed...
        SET PROPERTY OF lo_border 'WEIGHT' = 3. "max = 4
        FREE OBJECT lo_border.
      ENDIF.
    ENDIF.

    IF i_wraptext EQ 'X'.
      DATA(lv_wrap) = iv_row && ':' && iv_row.
      CALL METHOD OF io_excel 'Rows' = ls_rows
       EXPORTING
         #1 = lv_wrap.
      SET PROPERTY OF ls_rows 'WrapText' = 1.
    ENDIF.


  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD set_format.
************************************************************************
* @Importing@  I_BGNROW  -> Begin row
* @Importing@  I_BGNCOL  -> Begin column
* @Importing@  I_ENDROW  -> End row
* @Importing@  I_ENDCOL  -> End column
* @Importing@  I_FNAME   -> Font name (family)
* @Importing@  I_FCOLOR  -> Font color
* @Importing@  I_FBOLD   -> Font bold
* @Importing@  I_FSIZE   -> Font size
* @Importing@  I_FITLC   -> Font italic
* @Importing@  I_FULINE  -> Font underline
* @Importing@  I_BCOLOR  -> Background color
* @Importing@  I_MERGE   -> Merge Cells
* @Importing@  I_NFDEC   -> Number format (ex:'0.00')
************************************************************************
    DATA: ls_cellbgn  TYPE ole2_object,
          ls_cellend  TYPE ole2_object,
          ls_range    TYPE ole2_object,
          ls_font     TYPE ole2_object,
          ls_interior TYPE ole2_object.
    " Select All
    IF i_bgnrow EQ 0 AND
       i_bgncol EQ 0 AND
       i_endrow EQ 0 AND
       i_endcol EQ 0.
      CALL METHOD OF io_excel 'Cells' = ls_range.
    ELSE.
      " Select Range
      CALL METHOD OF io_excel 'Cells' = ls_cellbgn
        EXPORTING
          #1 = i_bgnrow
          #2 = i_bgncol.
      CALL METHOD OF io_excel 'Cells' = ls_cellend
        EXPORTING
          #1 = i_endrow
          #2 = i_endcol.
      CALL METHOD OF io_excel 'Range' = ls_range
        EXPORTING
          #1 = ls_cellbgn
          #2 = ls_cellend.
    ENDIF.

    CALL METHOD OF ls_range 'Select'.
    GET PROPERTY OF ls_range 'Font' = ls_font.
    " Font Name
    IF i_fname IS SUPPLIED.
      SET PROPERTY OF ls_font 'Name' = i_fname.
    ENDIF.
    " Font Color
    IF i_fcolor IS SUPPLIED.
      SET PROPERTY OF ls_font 'Color' = i_fcolor.
    ENDIF.
    " Font Bold
    IF i_fbold IS SUPPLIED.
      SET PROPERTY OF ls_font 'Bold' = i_fbold.
    ENDIF.
    " Font Size
    IF i_fsize IS SUPPLIED.
      SET PROPERTY OF ls_font 'Size' = i_fsize.
    ENDIF.
    " Font Italic
    IF i_fitlc IS SUPPLIED.
      SET PROPERTY OF ls_font 'Italic' = i_fitlc.
    ENDIF.
    " Font Underline
    IF i_fuline IS SUPPLIED.
      SET PROPERTY OF ls_font 'Underline' = i_fuline.
    ENDIF.
    " Background Color
    IF i_bcolor IS SUPPLIED.
      GET PROPERTY OF ls_range 'Interior' = ls_interior.
      SET PROPERTY OF ls_interior 'Color' = i_bcolor.
    ENDIF.
    " Merge
    IF i_merge IS SUPPLIED.
      CALL METHOD OF ls_range 'Merge'.
    ENDIF.
    " Number Format
    IF i_nfdec IS SUPPLIED.
*    " e.g.
      " NumberFormat = "General"
      " NumberFormat = "0.00","0.000","0" (Decimal)
      " NumberFormat = "#,##0.00 $","#,##0 $" (Currency)
      " NumberFormat = "dd/mm/yyyy;@","dd/mm/yy;@","[$-41F]d mmmm yyyy;@" (Date)
      " NumberFormat = "hh:mm;@","hh:mm:ss;@" (Time)
      " NumberFormat = "0.00%" (Percentage)
      SET PROPERTY OF ls_range 'NumberFormat' = i_nfdec.
    ENDIF.

    IF i_csize IS SUPPLIED.
      SET PROPERTY OF ls_range 'Columnwidth' = i_csize.
    ENDIF.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD set_border.
************************************************************************
* @Importing@  I_BGNROW  -> Begin row
* @Importing@  I_BGNCOL  -> Begin column
* @Importing@  I_ENDROW  -> End row
* @Importing@  I_ENDCOL  -> End column
* @Importing@  I_TRBLI   -> Top-Right-Bottom-Left-Inside (ex:01010)
************************************************************************
    DATA: ls_cellbgn TYPE ole2_object,
          ls_cellend TYPE ole2_object,
          ls_range   TYPE ole2_object,
          ls_borders TYPE ole2_object,
          ls_rows    TYPE ole2_object.
    " Select All
    IF i_bgnrow EQ 0 AND
       i_bgncol EQ 0 AND
       i_endrow EQ 0 AND
       i_endcol EQ 0.
      CALL METHOD OF io_excel 'Cells' = ls_range.
    ELSE.
      " Select Range
      CALL METHOD OF io_excel 'Cells' = ls_cellbgn
        EXPORTING
          #1 = i_bgnrow
          #2 = i_bgncol.
      CALL METHOD OF io_excel 'Cells' = ls_cellend
        EXPORTING
          #1 = i_endrow
          #2 = i_endcol.
      CALL METHOD OF io_excel 'Range' = ls_range
        EXPORTING
          #1 = ls_cellbgn
          #2 = ls_cellend.
    ENDIF.
    CALL METHOD OF ls_range 'Select'.
    " Top
    IF i_trbli(1) NE ''.
      CALL METHOD OF ls_range 'Borders' = ls_borders
        EXPORTING
          #1 = '8'. " xlEdgeTop
      SET PROPERTY OF ls_borders 'LineStyle' = 1.
      SET PROPERTY OF ls_borders 'Weight' = i_trbli(1).
    ENDIF.
    " Right
    IF i_trbli+1(1) NE ''.
      CALL METHOD OF ls_range 'Borders' = ls_borders
        EXPORTING
          #1 = '10'. "xlEdgeRight
      SET PROPERTY OF ls_borders 'LineStyle' = i_trbli+1(1).
      IF i_endcoll IS NOT INITIAL AND iv_right EQ 'X'..
        SET PROPERTY OF ls_borders 'Weight' = i_endcoll.
      ELSE.
        SET PROPERTY OF ls_borders 'Weight' = i_trbli(1).
      ENDIF.
    ENDIF.
    " Bottom
    IF i_trbli+2(1) NE ''.
      CALL METHOD OF ls_range 'Borders' = ls_borders
        EXPORTING
          #1 = '9'. "xlEdgeBottom
      SET PROPERTY OF ls_borders 'LineStyle' = i_trbli+2(1).
      SET PROPERTY OF ls_borders 'Weight' = i_trbli(1).
    ENDIF.
    " Left
    IF i_trbli+3(1) NE ''.
      CALL METHOD OF ls_range 'Borders' = ls_borders
        EXPORTING
          #1 = '7'. "xlEdgeLeft
      SET PROPERTY OF ls_borders 'LineStyle' = i_trbli+3(1).
      IF i_endcoll IS NOT INITIAL AND iv_left EQ 'X'.
        SET PROPERTY OF ls_borders 'Weight' = i_endcoll.
      ELSE.
        SET PROPERTY OF ls_borders 'Weight' = i_trbli(1).
      ENDIF.
    ENDIF.
    " Inside
    IF i_trbli+4(1) NE ''.
      " Vertical
      CALL METHOD OF ls_range 'Borders' = ls_borders
        EXPORTING
          #1 = '11'. "xlInsideVertical
      SET PROPERTY OF ls_borders 'LineStyle' = i_trbli+4(1).
      " Horizontal
      CALL METHOD OF ls_range 'Borders' = ls_borders
        EXPORTING
          #1 = '12'. "xlInsideHorizontal
      SET PROPERTY OF ls_borders 'LineStyle' = i_trbli+4(1).
    ENDIF.

    IF i_wraptext EQ 'X'.
      DATA(lv_wrap) = i_bgnrow && ':' && i_bgnrow.
      CALL METHOD OF io_excel 'Rows' = ls_rows
       EXPORTING
         #1 = lv_wrap.
      SET PROPERTY OF ls_rows 'WrapText' = 1.
    ENDIF.
    IF iv_center EQ 'X'.
      SET PROPERTY OF ls_range 'HorizontalAlignment' = 3.
    ENDIF.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD set_line_border.
************************************************************************
* @Importing@  I_BGNROW  -> Begin row
* @Importing@  I_BGNCOL  -> Begin column
* @Importing@  I_ENDROW  -> End row
* @Importing@  I_ENDCOL  -> End column
* @Importing@  I_TRBLI   -> Top-Right-Bottom-Left-Inside (ex:01010)
************************************************************************
    DATA: ls_cellbgn TYPE ole2_object,
          ls_cellend TYPE ole2_object,
          ls_range   TYPE ole2_object,
          ls_borders TYPE ole2_object,
          ls_rows    TYPE ole2_object.
    " Select All
    IF i_bgnrow EQ 0 AND
       i_bgncol EQ 0 AND
       i_endrow EQ 0 AND
       i_endcol EQ 0.
      CALL METHOD OF io_excel 'Cells' = ls_range.
    ELSE.
      " Select Range
      CALL METHOD OF io_excel 'Cells' = ls_cellbgn
        EXPORTING
          #1 = i_bgnrow
          #2 = i_bgncol.
      CALL METHOD OF io_excel 'Cells' = ls_cellend
        EXPORTING
          #1 = i_endrow
          #2 = i_endcol.
      CALL METHOD OF io_excel 'Range' = ls_range
        EXPORTING
          #1 = ls_cellbgn
          #2 = ls_cellend.
    ENDIF.
    CALL METHOD OF ls_range 'Select'.

    " Right
    IF iv_right EQ 'X'.
      IF i_trbli+1(1) NE ''.
        CALL METHOD OF ls_range 'Borders' = ls_borders
          EXPORTING
            #1 = '10'. "xlEdgeRight
        SET PROPERTY OF ls_borders 'LineStyle' = i_trbli+1(1).
        IF i_endcoll IS NOT INITIAL.
          SET PROPERTY OF ls_borders 'Weight' = i_endcoll.
        ELSE.
          SET PROPERTY OF ls_borders 'Weight' = i_trbli(1).
        ENDIF.
      ENDIF.
    ENDIF.
    " Left
    IF iv_right EQ 'X'.
      IF i_trbli+1(1) NE ''.
        CALL METHOD OF ls_range 'Borders' = ls_borders
          EXPORTING
            #1 = '7'. "xlEdgeLeft
        SET PROPERTY OF ls_borders 'LineStyle' = i_trbli+1(1).
        IF i_endcoll IS NOT INITIAL.
          SET PROPERTY OF ls_borders 'Weight' = i_endcoll.
        ELSE.
          SET PROPERTY OF ls_borders 'Weight' = i_trbli(1).
        ENDIF.
      ENDIF.
    ENDIF.
  ENDMETHOD.
  
ENDCLASS.


" I am successfully uploading the logo to Excel this way.

 METHOD excel_logo.

    TYPES :
      BEGIN OF ty_graphic ,
        line TYPE char255,
      END OF ty_graphic .

    DATA: l_bytecount TYPE i,
          lv_rc       TYPE i,
          l_rc        TYPE i,
          lv_content  TYPE xstring,
          gt_graphic  TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ty_graphic,
          l_content   TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bapiconten.

    DATA: graphic_size TYPE i.
    DATA: ws_filename  TYPE string VALUE 'C:\temp\logo.bmp'.
    DATA: l_image_path TYPE string          VALUE 'C:\temp\logo.bmp'.
    DATA: shapes TYPE ole2_object.
    DATA: lv_name TYPE  stxbitmaps-tdname.

    IF iv_logo IS NOT INITIAL.

      CALL FUNCTION 'SAPSCRIPT_GET_GRAPHIC_BDS'
        EXPORTING
          i_object       = 'GRAPHICS'
          i_name         = iv_logo
          i_id           = 'BMAP'
          i_btype        = 'BCOL'
        IMPORTING
          e_bytecount    = l_bytecount
        TABLES
          content        = l_content
        EXCEPTIONS
          not_found      = 1
          bds_get_failed = 2
          bds_no_content = 3
          OTHERS         = 4.

      IF sy-subrc NE 0.
        MESSAGE ID sy-msgid TYPE sy-msgty NUMBER sy-msgno
        WITH sy-msgv1 sy-msgv2 sy-msgv3 sy-msgv4.
      ENDIF.

      CALL FUNCTION 'SAPSCRIPT_CONVERT_BITMAP'
        EXPORTING
          old_format               = 'BDS'
          new_format               = 'BMP'
          bitmap_file_bytecount_in = l_bytecount
        IMPORTING
          bitmap_file_bytecount    = graphic_size
        TABLES
          bds_bitmap_file          = l_content
          bitmap_file              = gt_graphic
        EXCEPTIONS
          OTHERS                   = 1.

      CALL FUNCTION 'SCMS_BINARY_TO_XSTRING'
        EXPORTING
          input_length = 34565
        IMPORTING
          buffer       = lv_content
        TABLES
          binary_tab   = gt_graphic
        EXCEPTIONS
          failed       = 1
          OTHERS       = 2.
      IF sy-subrc <> 0.
        MESSAGE ID sy-msgid TYPE sy-msgty NUMBER sy-msgno
                   WITH sy-msgv1 sy-msgv2 sy-msgv3 sy-msgv4.
      ENDIF.

      CONCATENATE 'C:\temp\' iv_logo '.bmp' INTO ws_filename.

      CALL FUNCTION 'GUI_DOWNLOAD'
        EXPORTING
          filename                = ws_filename
          filetype                = 'BIN'
        TABLES
          data_tab                = gt_graphic
        EXCEPTIONS
          file_write_error        = 1
          no_batch                = 2
          gui_refuse_filetransfer = 3
          invalid_type            = 4
          no_authority            = 5
          unknown_error           = 6
          header_not_allowed      = 7
          separator_not_allowed   = 8
          filesize_not_allowed    = 9
          header_too_long         = 10
          dp_error_create         = 11
          dp_error_send           = 12
          dp_error_write          = 13
          unknown_dp_error        = 14
          access_denied           = 15
          dp_out_of_memory        = 16
          disk_full               = 17
          dp_timeout              = 18
          file_not_found          = 19
          dataprovider_exception  = 20
          control_flush_error     = 21
          OTHERS                  = 22.

      DATA:
        cl_fxs_image_info TYPE REF TO cl_fxs_image_info.

      cl_fxs_image_info = NEW #( ).

      cl_fxs_image_info->determine_info( EXPORTING iv_data = lv_content
        IMPORTING ev_mimetype = DATA(l_ev_mimetype)
                  ev_xres     = DATA(l_ev_xres)
                  ev_yres     = DATA(l_ev_yres)
                  ev_xdpi     = DATA(l_ev_xdpi)
                  ev_ydpi     = DATA(l_ev_ydpi)
                  ev_bitdepth = DATA(l_ev_bitdepth) ).


      GET PROPERTY OF  co_sheet 'Shapes' = shapes.

      IF iv_opt EQ 'X'. " If fixed
        l_ev_xres = '180'.
        l_ev_yres = '67'.
      ENDIF.

      IF iv_logo NE 'ZLOGO'. "SE78
        l_ev_xres = 180 * 75 / 100.
        l_ev_yres = 67 * 75 / 100.
      ENDIF.

      CALL METHOD OF shapes 'AddPicture'
        EXPORTING
          #1 = ws_filename "Image file on the presentation server
          #2 = 1
          #3 = 1
          #4 = iv_satir "Left
          #5 = iv_sutun "Top
          #6 = l_ev_xres "Width
          #7 = l_ev_yres. "Height


      cv_sutun = l_ev_xres.

      CALL METHOD cl_gui_frontend_services=>file_delete
        EXPORTING
          filename           = ws_filename
        CHANGING
          rc                 = l_rc
        EXCEPTIONS
          file_delete_failed = 1
          cntl_error         = 2
          OTHERS             = 3.
    ENDIF.
  ENDMETHOD.

entry.title: How to Build ABAP REST File Transfer with Error Handling?
entry.content:
Hello,

I prepared a technical document on how outbound REST API communication can be implemented in ABAP for file transfer scenarios.

The document covers the integration flow, payload preparation, request/response handling, logging, and error handling. My main question is whether this approach is technically aligned with ABAP best practices for building a reliable and maintainable outbound file transfer solution.

I would appreciate any feedback, especially on the communication design, logging strategy, and error handling structure.

Thank you.

Spoiler

entry.title: Adding new item to an existing PO failing with I_PUCHASEORDERTP_2 in S/4Hana Cloud
entry.content:
Hello All,

I have a requirement where i have to add new items with cost center to an existing PO. I am using below code. 
NOTE : ls_po_num-purchaseorderbycustomer has PO number that i want to update.
              ls_po_item-accountassignmentnumber has the last acc asst number.
              GL Account is fetched automatically by the system in the backend.

  DATA(lv_acc_no) = ls_po_item-accountassignmentnumber.
    LOOP AT lt_vbap_temp ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<ls_vbap>).
      lv_acc_no = lv_acc_no + 1.
      DATA(lv_cid)  = |{ cv_itmcid }{ lv_acc_no }|.
      DATA(lv_cid1) = |{ cv_itmacc }{ lv_acc_no }|.

      APPEND INITIAL LINE TO lt_purchase_order_item ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<ls_poi>).

      <ls_poi>-%tky-purchaseorder = ls_po_num-purchaseorderbycustomer.
      <ls_poi>-%target = VALUE #( BASE <ls_poi>-%target ( %cid = lv_cid
                                 material                  = <ls_vbap>-material
                                 plant                     = <ls_vbap>-plant
                                 orderquantity             = <ls_vbap>-ordqty
                                 purchaseorderquantityunit = <ls_vbap>-purchaseorderquantityunit
                                 purchaseorderitem         = <ls_vbap>-purchaseorderitem
                                 netpriceamount            = <ls_vbap>-netpriceamount
                                 documentcurrency          = <ls_vbap>-documentcurrency
                                 accountassignmentcategory = <ls_vbap>-accassigncat

                                 %control = VALUE #( material = if_abap_behv=>mk-on
                                                     plant = if_abap_behv=>mk-on
                                                     orderquantity = if_abap_behv=>mk-on
                                                     purchaseorderquantityunit = if_abap_behv=>mk-on
                                                     purchaseorderitem = if_abap_behv=>mk-on
                                                     netpriceamount = if_abap_behv=>mk-on
                                                     documentcurrency = if_abap_behv=>mk-on
                                                     accountassignmentcategory = if_abap_behv=>mk-on )
                                   ) ).

      APPEND INITIAL LINE TO lt_po_acc_assign ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<ls_acc>).
      <ls_acc>-%cid_ref = lv_cid.
      <ls_acc>-%tky-PurchaseOrder = ls_po_num-purchaseorderbycustomer.
      <ls_acc>-%tky-purchaseorderitem = <ls_vbap>-purchaseorderitem.
      <ls_acc>-%target = VALUE #( BASE <ls_acc>-%target ( %cid = lv_cid1
                                                          purchaseorderitem = <ls_vbap>-purchaseorderitem
                                                          accountassignmentnumber = lv_acc_no
                                                          costcenter = <ls_vbap>-costcenter
                                                          wbselementexternalid = <ls_vbap>-wbselement
                                                          "GLAccount = <ls_vbap>-glaccount

                                      %control = VALUE #( purchaseorderitem = if_abap_behv=>mk-on
                                                          accountassignmentnumber = if_abap_behv=>mk-on
                                                          costcenter = if_abap_behv=>mk-on
                                                          glaccount = if_abap_behv=>mk-on
                                                          wbselementexternalid = if_abap_behv=>mk-on )
                                   ) ).


    ENDLOOP.

    MODIFY ENTITIES OF i_purchaseordertp_2
        ENTITY PurchaseOrder
        CREATE BY \_purchaseorderitem FROM lt_purchase_order_item
        ENTITY purchaseorderitem
        CREATE BY \_purordaccountassignment FROM lt_po_acc_assign
        REPORTED FINAL(ls_reported)
        FAILED FINAL(ls_failed)
        MAPPED FINAL(ls_mapped).

    IF ls_failed-purchaseorder IS NOT INITIAL.
      LOOP AT ls_reported-purchaseorder ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<ls_reported_po>).
        RAISE SHORTDUMP TYPE zcx_bc_message_wrapper
          USING MESSAGE.
      ENDLOOP.
    ENDIF.

While using this code i am continuously getting error " Numbering of child object does not match parent object (&1)." in ls_reported, only for PurchaseOrderItem - item 00030.

How can i solve this error.

BR.
Tushar

entry.title: AI Search for Archived SAP KBAs: Integrating ABAP with Azure AI Search
entry.content:
Leaving SAP maintenance support does not mean losing SAP knowledge — but it does mean losing tools customers were used to.

The journey with an alternative support provider typically begins with a custom archive created from SAP software and KBA notes downloaded from the SAP Support Portal before the support contract expires. 

While this archive contains valuable corrections and documentation, access to the content becomes the customer's exclusive responsibility. Only the customer is authorized to interact with the archived material as he has the right to download and keep the copy of SAP software (according to the license terms).

For many "former" SAP support customers, this creates a long-standing challenge as such content might hardly be utilized to its full without an appropriate toolkit.

Trivial tasks like implementing a single note with prerequisites can become unexpectedly complex. In addition, most modern search engines work best with standard document formats, while a significant portion of SAP knowledge is distributed in the proprietary format zipped and packed in the .SAR file.

As a result, customers often rely on simple text extracted from .txt or .pdf files which are downloaded from the SAP Support Portal and indexed using conventional search engines.
Unfortunately, these stripped-down versions lose formatting, embedded images and other contextual information that can be crucial for determining whether a KBA is relevant and lead to solving potential SAP bug or an issue.

The result is a degraded search experience where important insights may remain hidden simply because the original structure of the document is lost.

This "workaround" has many limitations. Yet for many "former" SAP support customers it gradually becomes a new norm — learning to live with compromises.

I decided to challenge this assumption.

Instead of accepting the workaround, I set out to turn it into a proper solution and establish a new "best practice" for searching of SAP KBA in customers' archives.

The result is a solution I call SAP Archive AI Search.

1. The system leverages Microsoft Azure AI Search as the backend search engine responsible for indexing and retrieving archived content.
2. As a middleware layer I used the ABAP SDK for Azure, which I extended to support Microsoft Azure AI Search APIs.
3. Finally, a custom ABAP client provides a seamless user experience inside SAP GUI, allowing users to submit queries, navigate results, and integrate directly with other Azure services such as Blob Storage for convenient access to archived files stored in the customer’s cloud environment.

Solution is infrastructure-less, you do not need to host and run search engine on your own, distributing indexed content across the users (clients) or even use 3rd party tools to attach your cloud storage to navigate and access the content from local machines.

Only thing you need is to search for the content, read and download whatever you need straight from your SAP Gui. 

Key capabilities.

  • Unified search experience across multiple archive sources (.SAR, .TXT, .PDF)

  • United rendering using SAP GUI HTML browser controls

  • Structured index following the SAP KBA layout (Label, Symptom, Reason, Solution, etc.) allowing to address each field/section with separate set of conditions for complex query scenarios

  • Preservation of the rich formatting contained in .SAR archives including additional technical information

  • Correction instruction, enabling delta-code navigation and where-used exploration

  • Validity search, scopes your search query based on application stack of your system

  • Faceted navigation, paging, and direct reference navigation for notes present in the archive

  • Relevance scoring, including term boosting and scoring profiles dramatically improves accuracy to your search conditions

  • Full support for Azure AI Search query syntax, enabling advanced scenarios such as:

    • searching thousands of notes in a single query

    • fielded search

    • proximity, fuzzy, and regular expression queries

    • grouped queries

  • Metadata search across indexed archive attributes

Examples:

  1. Simple search retrieves all matching records per your query highlighting all found terms/tokens 

     

  2.  List overview with direct navigation to correction instruction, attachments for download and additional metadata information


  3. Use multiple conditions to search relevant notes containing DB6 in the title related to tracing using negative search list per Component field and term boosting "unix" to rank matching records containing this term on top.
  4. Search within the "delta code" of your corrections and restrict the scope per remote application stack making sure you only look for what possibly can be implemented


  5. Use expert mode to search for multiple notes in the archive and return only titles for matching records


  6. Use advance techniques like regular expression, proximity and fuzzy search whenever you need it

     



    Note: search terms in maximum proximity of two tokens apart

  7. Direct reference note navigation for the KBA articles shifts the context of your original query, however you can still navigate back using return button (vertical navigation)

    Note: Reference navigation works only for the notes which exists in your archive. 

  8. Blob search mode, allows to search and navigate based on your metadata extracted out of your blob files

 

The goal of this project was simple: restore a powerful search experience for archived SAP knowledge for customers under the 3rd party support providers.

By combining ABAP development with modern cloud search capabilities, it becomes possible to turn static SAP archives into a fully searchable knowledge system again.

I hope this approach can inspire other developers facing similar challenges which may arise along your alternative support provider.

Would like to have such solution implemented in your SAP environment?  Feel free to contact me and I will share the access to the GitHub repositories.

entry.title: Message type SHPCON for outbound delivery change which has no picking process need to do batch split
entry.content:
Hi I'm using Basic type DELVRY03 and Message type SHPCON . Doing Outbound Delivery change which has no picking and packing process. Because sales order Outbound delivery. Need to do Batch Split while inbound idoc.
Currently i use E1EDL18 CHG BUT IT UPDATING ONLY THE HEADER DATA.
Batch split is not working but manually i can able to do batch split.
